    • Sustainability

Calculate Mould Growth Probability Using Various Models

Source: R/calcMould_models.R
calcMould_models.Rd

This function calculates the probability or critical conditions for mould growth based on temperature and relative humidity, using one of four different models.

Usage

calcMould_models(Temp, RH, model = c("Days", "Sedlbauer", "VIR", "Isaksson"))

Arguments

Temp

Numeric. Temperature (°Celsius)

RH

Numeric. Relative Humidity (0-100%)

model

Character. The model to use for calculation. Options are "Days", "Sedlbauer", "VIR", or "Isaksson".

Value

Numeric. The result depends on the chosen model:

  • Table: returns days to mould

  • Sedlbauer and VIR: Critical RH for mould growth from temperature

  • Isaksson: Daily dose (1/t(ms)) / D(rel/day)

Details

The results are very different so take care in interpretation.

Examples

calcMould_models(20, 80, "Days")
#> [1] 14
calcMould_models(20, 80, "Sedlbauer")
#> [1] 74.4
calcMould_models(25, 70, "VIR")
#> [1] 79.125
calcMould_models(22, 85, "Isaksson")
#> [1] 0.01162899


head(mydata) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    # Days_mould = calcMould_models(Temp, RH, "Days"),
    RH_crit_Sedlbauer = calcMould_models(Temp, RH, "Sedlbauer"),
    RH_crit_VIR = calcMould_models(Temp, RH, "VIR"),
    Mould_daily_dose = calcMould_models(Temp, RH, "Isaksson")
    )
#> # A tibble: 6 × 8
#>   Site   Sensor Date                 Temp    RH RH_crit_Sedlbauer RH_crit_VIR
#>   <chr>  <chr>  <dttm>              <dbl> <dbl>             <dbl>       <dbl>
#> 1 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:00:00  21.8  36.8              73.5        79.0
#> 2 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:15:00  21.8  36.7              73.5        79.0
#> 3 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:29:59  21.8  36.6              73.5        79.0
#> 4 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:44:59  21.7  36.6              73.5        79.0
#> 5 London Room 1 2024-01-01 00:59:59  21.7  36.5              73.5        79.0
#> 6 London Room 1 2024-01-01 01:14:59  21.7  36.2              73.5        79.0
#> # ℹ 1 more variable: Mould_daily_dose <dbl>
